gene_region.label: BDNF transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical BDNF genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: BDNF encodes brain-derived neurotrophic factor, a secreted neurotrophin that supports neuronal survival, synaptic plasticity, dendritic architecture, learning, memory, mood, and metabolic-neural crosstalk research.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local BDNF bundle is neuroplasticity and neuropsychiatric-research oriented. Common BDNF variants are trait modifiers and should not be treated as deterministic psychiatric or cognitive predictions.
variant_effect_overview: Val66Met is studied for activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, stress, mood, and exercise-response contexts.
Direction and magnitude are cohort, phenotype, and ancestry dependent.
condition_research_overview: Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: BDNF methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for BDNF.
Because BDNF biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use BDNF promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival.
Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000176697: BDNF coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: BDNF biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BDNF neurotrophin-plasticity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, mood/stress association studies, exercise-response biology, or TrkB pathway signaling. This is not a psychiatric or cognitive diagnosis.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) is curated as a BDNF marker for Synaptic plasticity and learning/memory research, Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context
research_context: Use this marker as a BDNF pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) with BDNF methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) or linked BDNF locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Synaptic plasticity and learning/memory research, Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) as a gene-specific marker for Synaptic plasticity and learning/memory research, Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context and neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival.
